steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most accurately compares the two discoveries. Both discoveries involve DNA mobility: McClintock found DNA segments that move within a genome; Lederberg found phage DNA that moves from virus into host cell. The correct answer must capture that shared element accurately.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Like...also' structure: correctly describes McClintock's moving DNA segments and shows Lederberg's phage DNA incorporation as a parallel. ✓
  • Choice B — reverses the scientists: attributes McClintock's work to Lederberg and vice versa. ✗
  • Choice C — conflates the discoveries: merges the two findings into one, losing the comparison entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — misattributes work: credits the wrong scientist with the wrong discovery. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
